questions.question: What is the time complexity of inserting a new node into an empty Binary Search Tree?
questions.explanation: Inserting a new node into an empty Binary Search Tree takes O(log n) time. This is because we need to traverse the tree until we find the correct position for the new node.

questions.question: What is the purpose of a Binary Search Tree?
options: To store data in a random order
To store data in a sorted order
To store data in a balanced order
To store data in an unbalanced order
questions.explanation: The purpose of a Binary Search Tree is to store data in a sorted order, allowing for efficient searching, inserting, and deleting of nodes.

questions.question: What is the time complexity of searching for a node in a Binary Search Tree?
questions.explanation: Searching for a node in a Binary Search Tree takes O(log n) time. This is because we need to traverse the tree until we find the correct node.

questions.question: What is the time complexity of finding the minimum value in a Binary Search Tree?
questions.explanation: Finding the minimum value in a Binary Search Tree takes O(1) time. This is because we can start at the root node and keep going left until we find the minimum value.

questions.question: What is the time complexity of finding the maximum value in a Binary Search Tree?
questions.explanation: Finding the maximum value in a Binary Search Tree takes O(1) time. This is because we can start at the root node and keep going right until we find the maximum value.
